amend_note: All commit-hash fields above are the on-HEAD (post-amend) hashes. ~/.afterhours/hooks/post-commit rewrites every commit immediately after creation, so the hashes printed by `git commit` itself are not on `main`. These fields are re-resolved with `git rev-parse` after the post-commit hook runs. Stale (pre-amend) hashes that should NOT be referenced anywhere: 445c783, c3e1c21, 047fc83, 04fe06d, 07e6d88, f5f8265.
mission: Commit 1ad9441 added 4 SDLC artifacts (CLAUDE.md, SDLC.md, TESTING.md, ARCHITECTURE.md) + .gitignore lines to claude-gdlc-wizard — a wizard-distribution repo. The repo is NOT a game project; it IS a software project, so SDLC governs how the distribution machinery is built (per xdlc Interop Pattern). Verify the artifacts are honest, internally consistent, and ground-truth against the filesystem, test counts, and upstream version claims. Subsequent rounds (2, 3, 4) fixed Codex findings; round 4 is now under recheck.
success: Reviewer verifies in round 4: (1) §Hooks in SDLC.md no longer claims an active plugin path that doesn't exist on disk; (2) the compliance-block fail-fast structure (item 2 scratch check + item 6 honest-status check) actually catches the failure modes round-2/round-3 identified; (3) the handoff JSON is now structurally clean (no duplicate keys); (4) every cited commit hash is an ancestor of HEAD; (5) the live test suite still totals 94 assertions (run `rg -c '^test_[a-z0-9_]+$' tests/*.sh` and sum); (6) no AI-slop terms; (7) the round_history accurately reflects what each round did.
failure: Reviewer rubber-stamps without (a) running item 6 honest-status check against the current tree and confirming exit 0; (b) confirming there are no duplicate JSON keys via `jq -e '. as $o | reduce paths(scalars) as $p ({}; .[$p[0]] += 1) | to_entries | map(select(.value > 1))' .reviews/handoff-sdlc-bootstrap.json`; (c) verifying every hash in the JSON is an ancestor of HEAD; (d) checking that SDLC.md has no remaining active-plugin claims (the round-3 finding (b) STILL_OPEN was about *publishing-doc* claims, not just §Hooks).
verification_checklist: (a) Run the full compliance block (SDLC.md §Compliance verification) with RELEASE=sdlc-bootstrap. Confirm exit 0. If item 2 (scratch install) fails: real bug. If item 6 (honest-status) fails: stale CERTIFIED claim or stale commit hash somewhere.
(b) `jq . .reviews/handoff-sdlc-bootstrap.json >/dev/null` — must succeed, no parse errors, no duplicate-key warnings.
(c) For each hash field in the handoff JSON: `git merge-base --is-ancestor $HASH HEAD` must return 0.
(d) `rg -n 'sdlc-wizard-wrap/[^.]' SDLC.md TESTING.md ARCHITECTURE.md CLAUDE.md README.md` — every hit must be in the context of the disabled state, not an 'active' claim.
(f) `rg -nE '\b(delve|game-changer|elevate|deep dive|navigate|seamlessly|comprehensive|robust|landscape)\b' CLAUDE.md TESTING.md SDLC.md ARCHITECTURE.md` — no hits.

(g) Item 6 false-positive smoke test: temporarily inject `(sdlc-bootstrap CERTIFIED)` into a publishing doc (SDLC.md), re-run item 6, expect exit 1 with 'a publishing doc claims CERTIFIED'. Revert the injection. Confirms the check still works after the round-4 narrowing.

(h) Item 6 archive-immunity test: confirm `.reviews/codex-review-sdlc-bootstrap-r2.md` (which historically contains 'CERTIFIED after round 2' as quoted evidence) does NOT trip item 6, because the check is now scoped to publishing docs only.
review_instructions: Round 4 is the recheck of the handoff structure itself plus the item 6 honest-status check fix and the lingering active-plugin claims. The previous three rounds revealed pattern: each fix introduced a new edge case. Be paranoid about dogfooding — the handoff is the input to its own review, so structural problems compound. Do not certify if the handoff fails its own item 6 check.

round_history.findings_summary: FAIL on hook count off-by-one, stale agentic-sdlc-wizard ref, broken compliance bash glob, blast-radius self-exemption. PASS on stat claim + upstream version + AI-slop. CONCERN on test execution (sandbox blocked).

(b) STILL_OPEN — wrap was disabled outside session, SDLC.md's active-plugin claim went stale. (g) NEW_ISSUE — `|| true` masked init failures. (j) NEW_ISSUE — review-machinery exemption let false CERTIFIED claim slip into ARCHITECTURE.md. Traceability: original 047fc83 not on HEAD; 0d7bfa5 is.

(b) STILL_OPEN — SDLC.md had 4 lingering active-plugin claims outside §Hooks (Wizard Version row + §Skills heading + References footer). (g) CLOSED — fail-fast structure works. (j) NEW_ISSUE — item 6 honest-status check has 4 bugs: (1) `// empty` only applied to last jq field; (2) grep too broad — matched archived review-machinery; (3) handoff JSON had duplicate `round_2_commit` keys; (4) compliance block read the stale duplicate.

(b) STILL_OPEN — SDLC.md:10 still contained the bare path string `sdlc-wizard-wrap/` (in 'originally' parenthetical), tripping the round-3 grep `sdlc-wizard-wrap/[^.]` even though the surrounding wording was correct DISABLED context. Item 6 honest-status: CLOSED. Handoff structural cleanup: CLOSED. False-positive smoke: CLOSED with sandbox caveat (Codex couldn't mutate files but ran a non-mutating equivalent that fired the right error).

Content-clean: bare-path grep empty, item 6 PASS, handoff JSON clean, hashes all ancestors, conventional-commit OK. But the prompt's check 6 used `rg -nEi` which fails on ripgrep 15.1.0 (treats -E as encoding). Codex ran the equivalent commands (`rg -ni`, `grep -nEi`) and both returned empty — the underlying content passes. Strict by Codex's own standard, no certification on a malformed check command.
